Alright, read through it. Here's what I think:

The core design law is solid — separating durable expertise from volatile facts into weights vs retrieval is exactly right. Training patch notes or current stats into a LoRA is suicide anyway since you're retraining every time shit changes and you get hallucination city. Keeping that in LanceDB is smart.

Two things I want to flag though:

The equip cost model with instant vs heavy swap is realistic for llama.cpp's constraints, but sixty seconds of dark time mid-session is brutal. You need a warm standby strategy where the next likely loadout preloads in the background before Cole actually needs it. Otherwise we're going to have awkward pauses every time I switch domains.

The trigger system with keywords and intents feels fragile at scale. Once you've got more than three or four experts loaded, keyword overlap is going to cause routing collisions hard. You'll need a priority/confidence scoring layer in the decision faculty so it can disambiguate when two loadouts fire on the same prompt. Right now it's just "match or do
